p1_vlm_decision.reasoning: The bed is correctly oriented when its functional sleeping surface (mattress area) is horizontal and facing upward, and the headboard is vertical and at the head end. G5 shows the bed lying flat on its side, with the mattress surface horizontal but the headboard not upright. G6 shows the bed in a natural upright position: the mattress surface is horizontal and facing up, the headboard is vertical and at the head, and the base rests naturally on the ground plane. The other groups (G1–G4) show the bed tilted or rotated such that the sleeping surface is not horizontal or the headboard is not properly oriented. Therefore, G6 is the only group with the bed in a correct upright standing position.

p2_vlm_decision.reasoning: The front face of a bed is typically the headboard side, which is the taller and more decorative structure. In Angle 1 (0°), the headboard is clearly visible and facing the camera directly. In Angle 2 (90°), the side of the bed is shown, with the headboard on the left. In Angle 3 (180°), the foot of the bed is facing the camera, showing the opposite end. In Angle 4 (270°), the side of the bed is shown again, with the headboard on the right. Therefore, Angle 1 shows the front face directly facing the camera.
